description:
You are assisting a cloud-architect who is migrating some legacy services to a new environment.  
A legacy INI file already exists at  

/home/user/projects/cloud_migration/configs/legacy_service.ini  

Your job is to quickly inspect that file from the command line and generate a one-page, plain-text migration summary that the architect will feed into an automated pipeline.

The required output file and its exact location:  

/home/user/projects/cloud_migration/migration_summary.txt  

Required *exact* contents of migration_summary.txt (including case, ordering, spacing, and newline placement):

DB_HOST=legacy-db.internal  
DB_PORT=5432  
API_ENDPOINT=https://legacy-api.internal/v1  
API_TIMEOUT=30  
SECTION_COUNT=2  

Notes & constraints the test-suite will check:

1. The file must consist of exactly 5 non-empty lines, in the order shown above, each terminated with a single UNIX newline (line-feed, “\n”).  
2. No leading or trailing blank lines are permitted.  
3. Keys must be uppercase, and there must be no spaces around the equals sign.  
4. SECTION_COUNT must equal the total number of INI sections present in legacy_service.ini.  
5. You may assume the INI file is already present and readable.  
6. Accomplish the task with a short series of shell commands (2–3 commands is sufficient).  

Produce the summary file in the specified location; do not modify any other files.  
When finished, you should be able to display the file with “cat /home/user/projects/cloud_migration/migration_summary.txt” and see the exact 5 lines listed above.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

1. Directory tree:
/home/user/projects/cloud_migration/
/home/user/projects/cloud_migration/configs/

2. File: /home/user/projects/cloud_migration/configs/legacy_service.ini  
Exact contents:

[database]
host = legacy-db.internal
port = 5432
user = legacy_user
password = secret

[api]
endpoint = https://legacy-api.internal/v1
timeout = 30


Target state after successful completion:

File: /home/user/projects/cloud_migration/migration_summary.txt  
Exact contents (each line ends with '\n'):

DB_HOST=legacy-db.internal
DB_PORT=5432
API_ENDPOINT=https://legacy-api.internal/v1
API_TIMEOUT=30
SECTION_COUNT=2

Permissions: regular file, owner writable (default umask OK).

No other files or directories should be altered or added.
